title: Post-workout British Chives Crumble
description: A hearty, crumbly, post-workout British dish featuring chives, perfect for a refreshing snack after a workout. Inspired by traditional Yorkshire cuisine, this recipe is shojin-friendly and packed with protein.

ingredients.name: Cheddar cheese substitute (such as nutritional yeast)

instructions: Mix the oat flour, nutritional yeast, salt, and baking powder in a bowl.
Add the ghee or vegan butter to the bowl and mix until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
Stir in the chopped chives and mix until they are evenly distributed.
Transfer the mixture to a baking dish and press it down to form a crust.
Bake the crust in the preheated oven for 15 minutes, or until it is lightly golden brown.
While the crust is baking, prepare the filling by mixing the vegetable oil, lemon juice, garlic powder, and chopped parsley in a bowl.
After the crust has baked for 15 minutes, remove it from the oven and top it with the filling.
Return the dish to the oven and bake for an additional 10-15 minutes, or until the filling is heated through and the crust is crispy.
Remove the dish from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
Serve the post-workout British chives crumble warm, garnished with additional chopped chives if desired.
Store any leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
Reheat leftover crumble in the microwave or oven before serving.
